perPress.label: datadog-failover
perPress.answerPreview:
## Approach
The classic approach uses a hash map to store each number's index while scanning once. For every element, compute `target, num` and check if that complement exists in the map. If yes, return both indices; otherwise, store the current number and continue. This is O(n) instead of the O(n²)
